world_assessment: Extremely thin macro news week — crypto volatility dominated headlines with zero portfolio relevance. No major economic data, no Fed signals, no legislative catalysts. Portfolio-relevant items are corporate-level noise: UNH/Optum expanding strategically (modestly positive for Healthcare Aging thesis), MSFT/GS collaborating on low-emission software (minor positive), GOOGL facing Russia fines and India social media risk (manageable). The market is digesting Q1 earnings and consolidating. With no catalyst, this is a maintenance week — assess momentum in existing positions and make tactical adjustments where signals have degraded.

thesis_updates.notes: RSI=64, MACD bullish, OBV rising — three signals aligned. ADX=19 is low (below confirmed-trend threshold of 25), which is the one concern. Near upper BB suggests near-term resistance. However, the structural semiconductor thesis is unchanged — AI compute demand is accelerating and CUDA moat remains unassailable. Up 11% from entry. Hold with conviction; ADX weakness is a timing note, not a thesis break.

Price $367.40 — within $8 of our $375 target. Critically: ADX has declined from 38 at entry to 22 now (16 points), and MACD has turned bearish. This is approaching Lesson 6 trigger conditions (price near target + ADX decline >20 + MACD bearish). Not there yet — ADX decline is only 16 points and price hasn't cleared the target. Hold but prepare for exit within the next 1-2 reviews if price reaches $375 with continued ADX weakness. Do not get greedy past the target.

Concern: ADX dropped from 40 at entry to 17 now — a 23-point decline, below the 18 threshold referenced in Lesson 7. OBV is falling. MACD bearish. However, Lesson 7 full trigger requires ADX decline >30 points (we're at 23) and price >15% below target (we're at 12.5% gap: $161.83 vs $185). Thesis remains structurally intact — rate environment is supportive and banking supercycle is real. Holding, but this is on watch. If ADX drops further and OBV continues falling, we may need to exit pre-target on momentum evaporation.

ADX has collapsed from 35 at entry to 9 — near-complete evaporation of trend momentum. MACD bearish. OBV is flat. Price at $237.71 vs $260 target. The Blue Wave infrastructure thesis is still intact and no legislative reversal has occurred. ADX decline is 26 points (shy of the 30-point Lesson 7 trigger), OBV is flat not falling, and price gap to target is 8.5% (below the 15% threshold). Not a forced exit yet, but this position is losing its case. Holding with close watch — any further OBV deterioration triggers re-evaluation.

NUE has blown past our original $85 target — now at $100.85, +46.4%. Critically, this does NOT trigger Lesson 6: ADX remains at 39 (essentially unchanged from entry at 37), and OBV is RISING. Lesson 6 specifically requires ADX to decline >20 points from entry. The institutional trend is fully intact. Raising target to $120. This is the portfolio's best performer and exemplifies Lesson 3: hold when ADX is strong and OBV rising, even when MACD is bearish. The steel supercycle thesis is playing out exactly as modeled.

ADX=42 and OBV rising — the strongest technical confirmation in the portfolio alongside NUE. UNH/Optum strategic expansion into Central New York validates the Healthcare Aging thesis in real time. MACD is bearish near-term but that is noise against ADX=42. Up 10.6% from entry. Target $440 is intact. Hold with high conviction.

OBV is falling and ADX=18 — below the confirmed-trend threshold of 25. The position is up 13.8% which is a solid gain, but institutional accumulation is clearly reversing. No strong news catalyst this week to refresh the thesis. With max positions at 15 and a superior opportunity available (CVS: ADX=41, OBV rising), rotating capital into higher-momentum names is the disciplined action. Exiting at a 13.8% gain before momentum deterioration erases it.

OBV falling is the one concern. RSI=54, MACD bullish, above SMA50 — generally constructive. ADX=21 is modest. The MSFT/Accenture/GS low-emission software collaboration this week is minor but positive signal that MSFT's enterprise relationships are deepening. Azure AI infrastructure thesis remains intact. Hold — OBV weakness alone, without ADX collapse or thesis invalidation, is not an exit signal.

Russia fines ($8M equivalent) and India social media ban risk are headline noise — immaterial to GOOGL's fundamental thesis at their scale. MACD bullish, above SMA50, RSI=63. OBV flat and ADX=19 are modest. Potential Cerner acquisition interest signals healthcare IT strategic expansion — optionality upside. Hold.

OBV rising despite bearish MACD — institutional accumulation continuing. ADX=19 is modest but trend intact. RSI=54 shows room to run. Elective procedure recovery as COVID normalizes is the near-term catalyst. Healthcare Aging thesis unchanged. Hold.

ADX=28 and OBV rising — one of the cleaner technical setups in the portfolio alongside NUE and UNH. MACD bullish. RSI=66 shows momentum without overextension. BlackRock's Authentic Brands IPO advisory involvement confirms the financial supercycle thesis. Hold.

ADX=39 and OBV rising — same institutional pattern as NUE and UNH. RSI=68 and MACD bullish. Novo Nordisk's GLP-1/diabetes drug pipeline is a multi-year secular compounder with zero policy dependency. Healthcare Aging at 5/5 conviction. Hold with conviction.

RSI=72 elevated, MACD turning bearish, but ADX=38 and OBV rising confirm the institutional trend. Near-term pullback is possible given elevated RSI and bearish MACD, but stops are ATR-based. LNG is a natural gas infrastructure play benefiting from global energy demand recovery. Hold — ADX and OBV alignment outweighs the bearish MACD signal per our core principles.

Short thesis intact: RSI=39, below SMA50, MACD bearish, OBV falling, near lower BB. This is exactly what a working short looks like. We are slightly underwater (-2%) due to minor price drift but technical confirmation of distribution is strong. No Buffett-tier investor has disclosed a long position (Lesson 8 trigger). Hold the short. The structural cord-cutting and 5G capex destruction thesis has not changed.

new_positions.thesis: CVS Health is the highest-conviction new entry available: ADX=41 with OBV rising and MACD bullish is a textbook four-signal institutional accumulation pattern — the same setup that defined our best entries (NUE at entry, UNH). CVS operates at the intersection of three structural growth drivers: (1) Pharmacy benefits management through Caremark is a non-discretionary, recurring revenue stream that grows with population aging and prescription drug volumes — the aging U.S. population directly drives this, (2) Aetna health insurance integration creates a vertically integrated healthcare model that benefits from ACA expansion under Biden's agenda — more insured lives means more prescription volume and care delivery, (3) MinuteClinics and HealthHUB locations position CVS as a primary care access point for the 70M+ underserved Americans who use retail clinics as their main healthcare touchpoint. This is a Healthcare Aging theme core position that complements UNH (managed care) and NVO (pharmaceutical manufacturing) with a different exposure vector: distribution, retail pharmacy, and insurance. CVS's scale creates purchasing power advantages in drug procurement that smaller operators cannot replicate. The technical setup is the strongest available among unowned names in our universe.
new_positions.invalidation: CVS exits below $74 (ATR-based stop). Thesis breaks if: (1) CMS substantially cuts Medicare Part D reimbursement rates beyond current expectations, reducing Caremark economics, (2) Amazon Pharmacy or a major competitor materially takes prescription market share with evidence of same-store script count declines, (3) Aetna integration produces $1B+ unexpected charges signaling structural execution failure.
new_positions.timing_note: ADX=41 with OBV rising and MACD bullish — all four institutional signals aligned. RSI=66 shows momentum without overextension. Replacing META (OBV falling, ADX=18) with CVS (OBV rising, ADX=41) is a clean momentum upgrade within similar portfolio allocation.

close_positions.reason: Momentum deterioration: OBV is falling (institutional distribution) and ADX=18 falls below the 25-threshold for confirmed trend. Up 13.8% — disciplined profit-taking before momentum evaporates further. Rotating into CVS which has ADX=41 and OBV rising — a materially stronger technical setup. No thesis invalidation, but capital earns better returns where institutional trend is confirmed.

theme_updates.name: Financial Re-rating
theme_updates.reason: Both GS and JPM are showing bearish MACD and declining ADX. The initial re-rating from Blue Wave is maturing — rates have partially normalized and the step-change in sentiment appears to have run its course. We remain long GS and JPM but the tailwind is less powerful. Downgrading from 4 to 3 to reflect theme maturation, not invalidation.

theme_updates.name: Healthcare Aging
theme_updates.reason: UNH/Optum strategic expansion news this week reinforces the structural thesis. NVO and UNH showing ADX=39-42 with OBV rising — institutional confirmation of the theme. Adding CVS this week further concentrates us here. Theme remains at 5/5 — our highest conviction call.

lessons: When a position significantly exceeds its price target but ADX remains high (>35) from entry and OBV continues rising, the correct action is to RAISE the target and hold — not exit. The Lesson 6 exit trigger explicitly requires ADX to DECLINE >20 points from entry. When ADX is stable or rising after a target breach, the institutional trend is accelerating, not decelerating. Trigger: price above original target + ADX flat or rising from entry + OBV rising = raise target and hold, do not exit. NUE exemplifies this: $85 target breached at $100.85, but ADX=39 (same as entry) and OBV rising — exiting would have forfeited continued upside on the portfolio's best performer.

lesson_updates.reason: NUE's continued strength beyond the $85 target provides the complementary evidence to Lesson 6: the ADX decline condition is load-bearing, not decorative. NUE is above target with ADX still at 39 and OBV rising — and the correct action (hold, raise target) is the opposite of Lesson 6's exit trigger. This validates that Lesson 6's specificity (ADX must decline >20 points) is precisely correct. The lesson is more actionable with this positive case alongside the cautionary DE example.

belief_updates.name: Thesis Integrity Over Price Action
belief_updates.description: When a position is in drawdown OR has exceeded its price target, the exit decision must be driven by institutional momentum signals — not price movement alone. The two-sided principle: (1) HOLD through drawdowns when ADX >25 with OBV rising and no fundamental thesis change — price weakness is noise, not signal; (2) HOLD through target breaches when ADX is flat/rising from entry and OBV is rising — the market is telling you the thesis has more runway. Exit only when: thesis breaks on fundamentals, OR ADX declines >20 points from entry AND MACD turns bearish AND price reaches target (Lesson 6), OR ADX declines >30 points AND falls below 18 AND OBV falls pre-target (Lesson 7). The unifying principle: price alone is never the exit trigger — institutional momentum signals are.

weekly_summary: Thin macro week — no major catalysts. Focused on portfolio maintenance: closed META (OBV falling, ADX=18, taking 13.8% gain) and rotated into CVS (ADX=41, OBV rising, Healthcare Aging theme). NUE continues to be the portfolio standout at +46.4% — raising target to $120 as ADX=39 and OBV rising confirm the steel infrastructure thesis is accelerating, not peaking. GS approaching $375 target with declining ADX — preparing for disciplined exit next review if target is reached. JPM and CAT flagged for momentum concerns (ADX declining) but structural theses intact. T short technical confirmation continues with OBV falling and RSI=39. Financial Re-rating theme downgraded from 4 to 3 as the initial re-rating move matures. Portfolio underperforming S&P by 1.5% — CVS addition and NUE's continued momentum should contribute to closing the gap.
